displayTitle: The Image Chain: Lens Trains to Pixels

beats.narration: Every borescope image crosses a chain from the objective lens to your eye or a screen. This lesson follows that chain.

payload.title: THE IMAGE CHAIN
payload.sub: How borescopes carry a picture from the probe tip to your eye or screen.

beats.narration: Three ways to move the image: a rigid lens train, a coherent fiber bundle, or a chip sending video up a wire.

payload.heading: Three ways to move the image

beats.narration: A coherent bundle keeps every fiber in the same position at both ends — each fiber is one pixel. Broken fibers show as permanent black dots.
On a CCD or CMOS sensor, photons free electrons at each photosite. Charge builds with brightness, and a converter turns every pixel into a number.
Any stage can degrade the picture, from objective to display. The image chain is only as strong as its weakest link.

payload.sub: Verify the chain end-to-end with a resolution target at your working settings.
